name: Italy

travel.typicalFlightTime: 2–3 hours by air (or long-distance train / driving via the Alps)
notes: Direct flights from Rome, Milan, and other hubs are common; some movers drive or use rail with connections.
Cross-border workers who keep main residence in Italy may follow different registration, tax, and social-security rules.

commonReasons: EU remote work and lifestyle moves

commonRoutes: Work, study, or self-sufficiency within EU rules
notes: Italian nationals do not follow the same MVV pathway as non-EU nationals for standard EU residence.
BRP registration, BSN, and practical setup are usually the main early focus.

commonStarterDocuments: Valid passport or Italian national ID (carta d'identità) where accepted
Employment or study documents where relevant
Italian civil-status documents when needed (some may need apostille per Netherlands Worldwide)
countrySpecificNotes: Netherlands Worldwide describes which Italian documents can be used immediately in the Netherlands and which need an apostille from the Italian authorities; official documents in Italian often need no translation for Dutch use.
Confirm each document with the organisation that requests it.

shipping.intro: Italy–Netherlands moves often combine air travel for people and bags with road transport or a removal company for larger households. Plan unloading, parking, and building access at your Dutch address.
notes: Compare air vs road options for volume, timing, and pet transport if relevant.
Temporary parking permits may be needed for unloading in Dutch cities.

seo.title: Moving to the Netherlands from Italy | Registration, Documents, Costs & Practical Setup
seo.description: Planning a move from Italy to the Netherlands? Explore EU move rules, registration, BSN, document requirements, housing, banking, and the practical steps to settle in.
keywords: moving to the netherlands from italy
eu citizen move to netherlands from italy
living in the netherlands as an italian citizen

faqOverrides.q: Do Italian citizens need a visa to live in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Italian nationals are EU citizens. For ordinary long-term residence based on EU free movement, you generally do not need a visa or standard residence permit in the same way as non-EU nationals. EU, EEA, and Swiss citizens can stay in the Netherlands for up to three months with a valid travel document; longer stays follow the EU rules described on the IND and Government.nl. You must still meet the conditions that apply to your situation—confirm on official pages.

faqOverrides.q: Do I need an MVV to move from Italy to the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: An MVV is used in many non-EU long-stay procedures. It is not the standard framework for Italian nationals exercising EU free movement for ordinary residence. Unusual cases may still need tailored checks—use official sources or professional advice if unsure.

faqOverrides.q: How do I register after moving from Italy?
faqOverrides.a: If you will stay in the Netherlands for more than four months, you normally register in the BRP through your municipality. Government.nl states you must register within five days of arrival. The municipality issues your BSN. See our municipality registration guide and the Government.nl BRP pages.

faqOverrides.q: Do Italian documents need translation for the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Netherlands Worldwide indicates that foreign official documents in Italian do not need to be translated for use in the Netherlands. Some documents can be used immediately; others may need an apostille from the Italian authorities. Always confirm for your specific document and the organisation that requests it.

faqOverrides.q: What should I prioritise in the first weeks?
faqOverrides.a: Most people focus on BRP registration and BSN, Dutch basic health insurance where required, a bank account, and DigiD. Housing search intensity is often the hardest part, not immigration paperwork.

faqOverrides.q: When might immigration lawyers or visa consultants still help?
faqOverrides.a: Complex family situations, verification against EU law, or specialised legal questions may warrant support. For typical EU moves, many people start with gemeente guidance and official web pages.

customNotes: EU movers from Italy often prioritise housing platforms and banking after registration planning.
